# Stelldyn

> Stelldyn is a boutique advisory and trading firm for the industrial materials sector. It helps producers, manufacturers, brands, recyclers, and private-equity firms buy, sell, and recover industrial materials and byproducts — including paper, pulp, recovered fiber, plastics, metals, timber, and chemicals — with an advisory-first, fully transparent model that aligns global supply with circular demand.

Stelldyn is based in Atlanta, Georgia, USA. It was founded by Josh Silcox, a supply chain and commercial executive with 15+ years leading sourcing, category strategy, supplier governance, and a global recycling business inside Fortune 500 manufacturing. The firm's differentiator is operator-grade expertise combined with a consulting-first stance: advice always precedes any transaction, and there are no hidden markups.

## Services
- [Advisory & Strategy](https://stelldyn.com/advisory): Market and pricing analysis, supply chain mapping, byproduct and circular-economy strategy, capability building, and advisory retainers. Outcome: measurable EBIT improvement.
- [Agency & Trading](https://stelldyn.com/agency): Transparent U.S. market representation for global producers — introductions to vetted buyers, sales agency support, trading facilitation, performance analytics, and compliance.
- [Services overview](https://stelldyn.com/services): Full-lifecycle material management across advisory, agency/trading, and digital connection & intelligence (traceability, HTS classification, SKU management).

- [Byproducts are a P&L line, not a cost](https://stelldyn.com/insights/byproducts-are-revenue-not-cost) — Jun 2026

## Who Stelldyn serves
Material producers, global brands, logistics and distribution operators, private-equity firms, and consulting/legal firms — across pulp/paper/recovered fiber, packaging and containers, plastics and polymers, metals and scrap, textiles, and agriculture and organics.

## Founder
Josh Silcox — Founder & Principal Advisor. Career track record includes leading a $1B+ direct-materials sourcing portfolio (pulp, paperboard, packaging, recycled materials, logistics) across 50+ manufacturing sites, 80+ suppliers, and 30+ countries; delivering $50MM+ in annual enterprise value and $17–28MM in annual EBIT improvement; and holding full P&L for a $100MM global recycling and industrial-packaging business. MBA (Strategy), Emory University Goizueta Business School; B.S. Business Management, Georgia Institute of Technology. LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/joshsilcox

## Contact
Email: info@stelldyn.com  ·  Location: Atlanta, GA, USA
